message:
fix: prevent writer semaphore token leak and add busy_timeout to readers

In writer() and concurrent_writer(), if FrankenConnection::open() or
apply_config() failed after recv() consumed a semaphore token, the token
was permanently lost (only WriterGuard::drop returns it). After enough
failures the writer pool would be exhausted and all writer() calls would
deadlock. Fix by returning the token to the channel before propagating
the error.

Also add PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000 to reader connections, matching the
writer config. Without this, readers return SQLITE_BUSY immediately on
WAL contention instead of waiting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
